diagrams.title: Medicine Manual Of Luang Pu Sukh
diagrams.genreLabel: Medicine · ตำรายา
diagrams.desc: A printed section-divider (not an illustrated diagram): a near-blank leaf carrying one bold centred word ภาคพิเศษ (phaak phiset, "special section / appendix"). It marks the start of the supplementary appendix in Luang Pu Sukh medicine manual, following the main body of remedies.

diagrams.title: Supreme Maha Mantra Katha
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A printed section-divider (not a hand-drawn diagram): a near-blank leaf carrying one line of modern Thai type centred on it — ยันต์หนีบ เข้าแท่งเมตตามหานิยม — opening the chapter on the yant nip (pinch/press) class of charms in the metta-mahaniyom (loving-kindness, great-popularity) family. Faint show-through of number-grid yant from adjacent leaves ghosts the margins. It marks where the manual tu

diagrams.title: Chinese Arts For Beauty And Wealth
diagrams.genreLabel: Other · อื่น ๆ
diagrams.desc: A single large brushed Chinese character 美 (měi, "beauty") in bold semi-cursive calligraphy, filling the leaf, captioned in Thai รูป ๑ ความงาม ("figure 1, beauty"). This opens the Chinese-arts book of auspicious lucky-words: the character itself is the charm, a brush-written wish for beauty to be displayed, hung or copied.

diagrams.title: Chinese Arts For Beauty And Wealth
diagrams.genreLabel: Other · อื่น ๆ
diagrams.desc: A single large brushed Chinese character 調 (tiáo, "harmony / attunement") in bold calligraphy, captioned in Thai รูป ๒ ความกลมกลืน ("figure 2, harmony / blending-together"). A lucky-word for concord and balanced relations, presented as brush-art to be displayed or traced.

diagrams.title: Chinese Arts For Beauty And Wealth
diagrams.genreLabel: Other · อื่น ๆ
diagrams.desc: Two brushed Chinese characters 桃花 (táohuā, "peach blossom") stacked vertically in bold calligraphy, captioned in Thai รูป ๓ ดอกท้อ ("figure 3, peach flower"). Peach blossom is the classic Chinese emblem of romance and love-luck (the taohua "peach-blossom fortune") — a charm-word for attraction and affairs of the heart.

diagrams.title: Chinese Arts For Beauty And Wealth
diagrams.genreLabel: Other · อื่น ๆ
diagrams.desc: Two brushed Chinese characters 勁竹 (jìng zhú, "vigorous / strong bamboo") in bold calligraphy, with a small Thai caption glossing it as bamboo (รูป ๔, ไผ่). Bamboo is the emblem of upright resilience — bending without breaking, strength through hardship — offered here as an auspicious lucky-word.

diagrams.title: Chinese Arts For Beauty And Wealth
diagrams.genreLabel: Other · อื่น ๆ
diagrams.desc: Two brushed Chinese characters 風柳 (fēng liǔ, "wind-willow / willow in the wind") in bold calligraphy, with a faint Thai caption (รูป ๗). The willow bending in the wind is an emblem of grace, suppleness and yielding strength — a lucky-word for graceful adaptability.

diagrams.title: Chinese Arts For Beauty And Wealth
diagrams.genreLabel: Other · อื่น ๆ
diagrams.desc: A vertical column of brushed Chinese characters read as an auspicious wealth-phrase (小/少 腹 大 富, "small means, great riches"), captioned in Thai รูป ๘ เงินน้อย โชคลาภ ("figure 8, little money — windfall fortune"). A prosperity charm-phrase promising great fortune from small beginnings, written as display calligraphy.

diagrams.title: Yantra Craft And Katha Lanna Lineage
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A tower-form yantra: a magic-number grid built up into the silhouette of a chedi (stupa), narrow at the crowning tier and widening through stepped registers to a broad base, with two short arms projecting left and right near the mid-body. Every cell holds a Tham Lanna numeral; read across and down, the rows and columns form the recited number-square (yang lek) whose totals carry the spell. A short

diagrams.title: Yantra Craft And Katha Lanna Lineage
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A sole-of-the-foot yantra: a single large footprint outline (rounded heel below, five toe-lobes above) with a Tham Lanna caption at the toes. A dotted seam runs down the centre dividing the sole into left and right fields, each filled with a descending column of Tham Lanna letters and numerals. The foot-form recalls the Buddhapada (Buddha footprint) repurposed as charm-ground: syllables written in

diagrams.title: Yantra Craft And Katha Lanna Lineage
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A caryatid yantra: a tall multi-tiered obelisk of stacked magic-number cells — triangular-hatched upper registers narrowing to a finial above a dense square grid — held aloft by a squatting frontal figure. The bearer (broad face, patterned loincloth, knees splayed, both arms raised overhead) lifts the whole number-tower on its hands, with a small syllable-cluster written across its chest. This is

diagrams.title: Yantra Craft And Katha Lanna Lineage
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A yant worksheet rather than a finished figure: four separate number-groups, each headed by a Tham Lanna numeral (1, 2, 3, 4), set out as small blocks of stacked Tham Lanna digits down the left, with cursive annotations in the right margin keying each group to its use. This is the reckoning behind a yant — the component number-sequences (yang lek) a practitioner assembles and totals before laying

diagrams.title: Yantra Craft And Katha Lanna Lineage
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A small figural drawing centred on the leaf: a seated rounded form — read as a meditating figure or a lotus-borne mound — from whose crown a segmented unalom spirals upward and breaks into a rising flame, with scattered droplet-marks to either side. Tham Lanna captions run above and below. The design is a devotional heart-yant: the coiled unalom and flame mark the rising of empowered breath or the

diagrams.title: Yantra Craft And Katha Lanna Lineage
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A figural heart-syllable yant: a symmetrical face-or-crown emblem — a central rounded visage with a small seed-glyph at its middle, framed by two great curling arms/horns that sweep outward and hook back like a crown or moustache. Loose Tham Lanna letters float above and to the sides. This is a hua chai (heart-of-the-katha) figure: the essential syllables of a spell compressed into one anthropomor

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A sak-yant tattoo stencil captioned in modern Thai ยันต์ดอกบัวทอง (yant dok bua thong, the golden-lotus yant). A single tall vertical column of Tham Lanna spell-script runs the length of the leaf like a spine tattoo, rising to a full-blown lotus blossom at the crown; above it three flame-spike unalom sceptres stand like antennae. The lotus marks purity and blossoming fortune; the script column is

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A sak-yant stencil captioned ยันต์สาริกาคู่ (yant sarika khu, the paired-sarika yant). Two facing sarika (myna) birds perch head-to-head atop a long vertical column of Tham Lanna spell-script, three unalom spikes rising above them. The sarika is the bird of sweet, persuasive speech: the paired form is a metta-mahaniyom charm for charm, eloquence and drawing affection — inked as a spine or nape col

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Two sak-yant stencils side by side. Left, captioned ยันต์ ๕ แถว (yant ha thaew, the five-row yant): five parallel vertical lines of dense Tham Lanna script, each capped by an unalom spike — the five-line kong-krapan protection grid. Right, captioned as a lattice yant: a diagonal crosshatch net filled with Tham Lanna numerals under five unalom, a woven number-mesh (turtle/net pattern) worked for en

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A sak-yant sheet of two money-bag roundels, captioned ยันต์ถุงเงินถุงทอง … โภคทรัพย์ (yant thung ngoen thung thong, the silver-bag gold-bag yant; phokha sap, wealth). Each is a drawn pouch: a quartered Na-square of four seed-syllables set inside a round bag, tied at the neck, ringed by a circle of Tham Lanna script and crowned with an unalom finial. A prosperity charm to draw and hold money — inke

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Sak-yant tracing stencil. Two "yant dok bua" (lotus yantras) drawn one above the other in fine black outline; caption below reads ๑ ยันต์ดอกบัว ฯ ("No. 1, Lotus Yantra"). Each yant is a stylised lotus blossom crowned by a flame-shaped unalome finial (tapering spire with a curled tip) flanked by small Khom finial ticks. A single Khom (ขอม) sacred-script syllable sits at the heart of each lotus; a c

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Sak-yant tracing stencil. A single composite yant: a central lotus with a flame/unalome finial and three spire-markers above (the middle one a rounded uposatha/spade marker), flanked left and right by a mirrored pair of winged birds (sarika/hamsa) whose plumes curl outward. A wide arc of Khom (ขอม) sacred-script syllables sweeps beneath the whole design. Caption reads ๑ ยันต์สาริกาคู่ ฯ ("No. 1, P

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc:
Sak-yant tracing stencil, a second "yant sarika khu" (paired-sarika) variant. A central lotus topped by a flame/unalome finial with three spire-markers rises between two facing sarika birds; a central vertical Khom column drops from the lotus, and a dense arc of Khom (ขอม) syllables curves along the base. Caption reads ๑ ยันต์สาริกาคู่ ฯ. Black outline stencil for tracing onto skin or cloth.
Tradi

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A sak-yant stencil captioned ยันต์นกยูง (yant nok yung, the peacock yant). Two peacocks with long sweeping tail-plumes stand facing, flanking a central tied money-bag/heart, the whole enclosed in a broad oval arc of Tham Lanna spell-script with a small unalom crown. The peacock is a metta-mahaniyom emblem of beauty, allure and radiant charm — a charm to be seen and favoured, traced across the back

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A sak-yant stencil captioned ยันต์หงส์คำ (yant hong kham, the golden-hamsa yant). A single hamsa (celestial swan/goose) rises with wings spread and a long curling scrolled tail, wrapped in an arc of Tham Lanna script with three unalom spikes above. The hong is a bearer of grace, high status and auspicious ascent — a metta-and-fortune charm inked as a rising figure on shoulder or back.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A sak-yant stencil captioned ยันต์พญาไก่ฟ้า (yant phaya kai fa, the lord-pheasant / firebird yant). A crested pheasant-phoenix sweeps across the leaf with a long trailing plumed tail, a small Na-circle and unalom at its crown, the body ringed and underscored by Tham Lanna spell-script. The kai fa is a metta-mahaniyom emblem of beauty, allure and charm — a stencil for attraction and favour, traced

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A sak-yant sheet of two butterfly stencils, captioned ยันต์ผีเสื้อ (yant phi suea, the butterfly yant). Above, a symmetrical open-winged butterfly with patterned wings, an unalom crown and an arc of Tham Lanna script; below, a single butterfly banked into a crescent of script. The butterfly is a metta-mahaniyom charm of beauty and attraction — worked to make the wearer alluring and beloved, inked

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A sak-yant stencil captioned as a Hanuman yant. Hanuman the white monkey-warrior leaps in three-quarter view, brandishing a staff/trident and a pennoned flag, mouth open, tail curling; his limbs and torso are covered in Na seed-syllables and the figure is wrapped in a sweeping arc of Tham Lanna script. This is a kong-krapan / invulnerability and power charm — Hanuman lends strength, boldness and i

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A sak-yant sheet of two ruesi (lersi) heads, captioned ยันต์เศียรฤๅษี พ่อแก่ (yant sian ruesi, pho kae — the head of the hermit-teacher, the Old Father). Above, a frontal face of the ascetic master crowned with a tiered headdress under three unalom spikes and an arc of script; below, a profile head of a bearded ruesi in a peaked tiger-skin cap. Pho Kae is the primordial teacher honoured in the wai

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A finely inked lotus blossom standing alone at the centre of an otherwise blank leaf: a full flower drawn in fine hatching, layered pointed petals opening around a beaded seed-pod crown, with a small closed bud rising at the top. No caption or spell-script — a decorative/devotional lotus frontispiece marking a section rather than a tattoo stencil, the lotus as emblem of purity and blossoming merit

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A small decorative vignette centred on a blank leaf: a symmetrical lotus flower rising from a paired base, flanked left and right by two fish (or fish-tailed birds) with feathery plumed sprays curling upward. No spell-script or caption — an ornamental pair-and-lotus motif (a prosperity/auspicious emblem of the twin fish and the flower) used as a section decoration rather than a working yant.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A decorative sak-yant vignette of two facing birds, drawn mirror-image with scaled bodies, upright crests and long dripping plumed tails that curl toward each other. No caption or spell-script — a paired-bird ornament of the metta-mahaniyom family (charm, coupling, sweet regard), a light figural motif for filling or bordering a tattoo rather than a formal yant.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A sampler leaf of six small charm motifs for tracing, ungrouped: top-left a Ganesha head crowned with an Om glyph (remover of obstacles, success); top-right a rearing naga serpent (water-lord, protection, fortune); centre a pair of fish — one ornate with plumed fins, one plain koi (abundance, fertility); bottom a small tied money-bag and a bottle-gourd/namtao (wealth, containment). These are the s

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Two blank money-bag templates for tracing, one above the other: the upper a round pouch with a lotus-frilled neck, its face ruled into an empty quartered grid ready to take a four-syllable Na-square; the lower a plain round bag tied with a bow, its face left empty. Unfilled outlines — the wealth-bag (thung ngoen thung thong) frame a practitioner completes by inking the seed-syllables into the quar

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Two gourd motifs: above, a small bottle-gourd (namtao) tied at the neck; below, a larger gourd with a tokay gecko (tukkae) climbing it, splay-toed feet gripping the swell, tail curling into a spiral. The gourd is a vessel of holy water and containment of fortune; the tukkae is a mahaniyom charm of allure and calling-in of wealth. Together a prosperity-and-attraction stencil.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Gecko charm stencils: above, two tokay geckos (tukkae) with star-splayed toes flanking a central lotus blossom, tails curling outward; below, a single gecko poised over a looped heart-shape. The tukkae is a metta-mahaniyom emblem — its call summons luck and admirers — and paired around the lotus it doubles the draw. Light figural motifs for attraction and popularity.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: More gecko stencils: above, two tokay geckos (tukkae) crossing tails in a symmetrical mirror, star-toed feet spread; below, a single gecko reaching toward a small round fruit or gourd it holds in its forefeet. Spare, elegant line drawings of the tukkae charm-animal — a mahaniyom sign of allure, calling-in and good luck, kept as small tattoo motifs.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Two lotus stencils in fine-line contemporary style, one above the other: the upper a pointed-petal lotus half-opened around a sharp central bud, the lower a rounder full-bloom lotus with a heart-shaped seed-pod. No spell-script or caption — clean decorative lotus flash of the modern sak-yant/tattoo repertoire, the lotus standing for purity, merit and unfolding fortune.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Two lotus motifs, finely hatched: above, an ornate frontal lotus rosette opened flat like a mandala around a beaded seed-crown; below, a closed lotus bud on a long curling stem that resolves into a small looped unalom-and-heart flourish at its tip. Decorative contemporary flash without spell-script — the lotus as emblem of purity and blossoming, the trailing unalom lending a devotional finish.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A symmetrical fine-line composition: a central lotus blossom flanked left and right by two ornate birds with long feathered sweeping plumes curling upward and back. No caption or script — a decorative lower-back / sternum style flash pairing the lotus (purity, fortune) with mirrored birds (metta, coupling, sweet regard), in the modern tattoo idiom.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A fine-line stencil: a full lotus blossom above, with two peacocks below facing inward, their long feathered tail-sprays fanning up and outward on either side. No script or caption — a decorative peacock-and-lotus flash, the peacock a metta-mahaniyom emblem of beauty and allure paired with the lotus of purity and blossoming fortune.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A fine-line stencil: a pointed-petal lotus at centre with two swallows/birds in flight below it, wings drawn as sweeping feathered plumes curling outward. No caption or spell-script — a decorative bird-and-lotus flash in the modern idiom, the paired birds carrying the metta sense of charm and coupling beneath the lotus.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A fine-line stencil: two peacocks facing inward flank a small round money-bag tied at the neck, their long ornate tail-feathers sweeping down and out in mirrored arcs. No caption or script — a decorative peacock-and-money-bag flash that fuses the peacock (beauty, allure, mahaniyom) with the wealth-bag (prosperity), a charm for both charm and fortune.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Two fine-line bird stencils: above, two long-tailed birds facing outward with scrolled plumed tails sweeping apart in mirror; below, two birds flanking a small bottle-gourd (namtao), their tails crossing and braiding beneath it. No caption or spell-script — decorative paired-bird flash of the metta-mahaniyom kind, the lower design tying the birds (charm, coupling) to the gourd (containment of fort

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Two fine-line stencils: above, two parrots/lovebirds set head-to-head so their bodies and folded wings form a heart (a love/coupling motif); below, a plump owl perched on a round orb or fruit, feathers finely scaled. No caption or script — decorative flash, the paired birds a charm for love and the owl a sign of watchful wisdom and, in the mahaniyom sense, drawing-in of fortune.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A single fine-line peacock standing with its tail fanned upright behind it in a spray of eyed plumes, body finely scaled, small crest on the head. No caption or spell-script — a decorative peacock stencil, the peacock a metta-mahaniyom emblem of beauty, radiance and allure, drawn as standalone flash for shoulder or back.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Two fine-line stencils of Thai mythical serpent-birds (naga- or makara-headed hong in the kranok idiom), each drawn in flight with a crested serpentine head, scaled body and flame-scrolled tail. No caption or script — decorative Lanna-ornament flash of the water/protection creatures, elegant filler figures rather than script-bearing yant.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A single fine-line phoenix/hong rising with wings spread wide and a long tail resolving into a cascade of ink-scroll spirals below. Small crest and open beak. No caption or spell-script — decorative flash of the celestial bird (grace, auspicious ascent, high status), drawn as a flowing standalone piece for back or thigh.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: An ornate fine-line firebird/phoenix in profile, wings and body finely scaled, with a great plumed peacock-eyed tail sweeping up and back in flame-like fronds. No caption or script — decorative flash of the auspicious bird, a metta-and-beauty emblem drawn as a flowing standalone tattoo rather than a script-bearing yant.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Two fine-line rooster stencils, one above the other: a proud cockerel (kai) with arched sickle tail-feathers and finely scaled breast, and below a second cock striding with raised hackles. No caption or spell-script — decorative fighting-cock flash, the kai an emblem of courage, valour and fighting spirit (kong / boldness), drawn as standalone motifs.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Two fine-line butterfly stencils: above, a symmetrical pair of open butterfly wings (drawn as a mirrored pair, split at the body); below, a single butterfly banked to one side with trailing scrolled lower wings. No caption or script — decorative butterfly flash of the metta-mahaniyom kind, a charm of beauty and attraction meant to make the wearer alluring.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Fish stencils in fine line: above, two round-bodied fish (gourami/pla) facing nose-to-nose with ruffled fins; below, a single koi-style carp swimming upward, body finely scaled. No caption or spell-script — decorative fish flash, the fish an auspicious emblem of abundance, fertility and flowing fortune, kept as filler or standalone motifs.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A single fine-line leaping deer (kwang) in mid-bound, its antlers stylised into curling flame-fronds, a patterned kranok band looping around its neck and haunch. No caption or spell-script — decorative deer flash; the kwang carries a metta-mahaniyom sense of gentleness and grace and recalls the deer of the Buddha first sermon, drawn as a flowing standalone piece.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A figural stencil of a four-armed deity astride Garuda: Phra Narai (Vishnu) crowned with a tiered mongkut, bearing discus, conch, club and trident in his hands, mounted on the winged bird-man Khrut whose wings spread wide and talons grip below. No script or caption — a divine mount motif (Narai song Khrut) carrying power, kingship and protection, drawn as a bold standalone figure for the back.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Two figural stencils of Hanuman the white monkey-warrior in acrobatic leaping poses, one above the other, mirrored diagonally. Each wears a crown and patterned loincloth, body finely tattooed with ornament, tail curling, one fist brandishing a trident/phra-khan. No caption or script — Ramakien warrior flash carrying kong-krapan (invulnerability), strength and boldness, drawn as bold standalone fig

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A figural stencil of a yaksha (giant) in the Ramakien idiom: a crowned, fanged demon-warrior striding with a long sword raised overhead in one hand and a bow held in the other, ornamented armour and sash, sinuous scroll-clouds below his feet. No caption or script — a guardian-giant motif carrying fierce protective power and the driving-off of harm, a bold back-piece figure.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: An intricate circular figural stencil: a fanged guardian face at the centre wrapped in a churning knot of naga serpents, banners and blades radiating outward, all densely ornamented. No caption or script — a knotted guardian/demon composition (a fierce protective emblem in the mural idiom), the coiling nagas and weapons packing power and menace into a roundel for the back or shoulder.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A figural stencil of Hanuman leaping, the crowned white monkey brandishing a trident in one raised hand and a pennoned banner-staff in the other, tail curling, body finely ornamented. No caption or script — Ramakien warrior flash carrying kong-krapan invulnerability and martial boldness, drawn as a dynamic standalone figure.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A figural stencil of a crowned deva/warrior astride a mythical lion-beast (rachasi/singha), holding aloft a long pennoned banner-flag, the mount rearing with flame-scrolled mane and tail. No caption or script — a deity-on-mount motif of rank, victory and protective power, drawn as a bold standalone figure.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A figural stencil of Hanuman the white monkey-warrior leaping, a sword raised in one hand, the other flung up, tail sweeping into an ornamented scroll beneath him, body crowned and finely patterned. No caption or script — Ramakien warrior flash carrying kong-krapan invulnerability, agility and boldness, a dynamic standalone back or arm figure.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A figural stencil of a crowned yaksha/deva warrior standing frontally on a woven knot of naga serpents, fanged face, ornamented armour and sash, a trident-blade in one hand. No caption or script — a guardian-giant figure treading the nagas, an emblem of dominating protective power and command over the serpent-world, a bold standalone back-piece.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A figural stencil of a crouching figure drawing a great bow that arcs into a heart-shaped loop around him; his whole body — chest, thighs, arms — is covered in spiral koh-marks like an all-over protective tattoo, and his face is beast-like. No caption or script — an archer/hunter guardian figure sheathed in spiral yant, an emblem of readiness, protection and covered-in-power (metaa/kong), drawn as

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A figural stencil of a striding tiger (suea) roaring, body fully striped, one forepaw raised with claws spread, tail lashing. No caption or script — the tiger is a paramount Lanna power-emblem carrying kong-krapan invulnerability, fierce authority (amnat) and command; a bold standalone back or chest figure, one of the great sak-yant beasts.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A figural stencil of a roaring tiger head (na suea), jaws open with bared fangs and lolling tongue, whiskers and ruff drawn as flaming fronds, striped brow. No caption or script — the tiger-face is a fierce power-and-authority emblem (kong-krapan, amnat, commanding presence), drawn as a compact standalone motif for shoulder or nape.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Two prowling-beast stencils: above, a striped tiger (suea) creeping head-down with tail trailing; below, a mythical lion-beast (singha/rachasi) in the mural idiom, flame-scrolled mane and clawed feet. No caption or script — both are power-and-protection emblems (the tiger for kong-krapan and authority, the singha for guardian strength), drawn as standalone figures.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A figural stencil of a mythical lion-beast (singha/rachasi) prowling, its dragon-like crested head thrown up, flame-scroll mane, ridged spine and clawed feet, tail curling into a flame-frond. No caption or script — a guardian-lion emblem of strength, ferocity and protective power in the Lanna mural idiom, drawn as a bold standalone figure.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A figural stencil of a Lanna guardian-lion (singha/rachasi) prowling in the mural idiom: crested dragon-like head with bared teeth and cross-hatched maw, flame-frond mane and ridged joints, clawed feet, tail curling into flame. No caption or script — a temple-guardian emblem of ferocity and protective power, drawn as a bold standalone figure for the back.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: Two serpent stencils: above, a single snake rising in an S-curve, its lower body looped into a heart; below, two snakes rearing head-to-head from an interlaced love-knot of their own braided bodies. No caption or script — naga/snake motifs binding protection with the heart-and-knot sense of union and holding-fast, decorative charm figures of water-power and bound affection.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A figural stencil of a ruesi (lersi) in profile: a bearded ascetic-sage with hooded brow and long goatee, crowned with a tall flame-tipped headdress and wearing a strand of prayer-beads across the bare shoulder. No caption or script — this is the hermit-teacher (a Pho Kae / ruesi likeness) honoured as master and protector in the wai khru, placed on the skin as the source-figure of the craft.

diagrams.title: Sak Yant Stencils For Tracing
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: A sheet of two teacher/deity heads: above, a frontal crowned face with a tiered pointed mongkut and calm features (a thewada/deva head); below, a profile bearded ruesi (lersi) in a flame-tipped crown with a long wispy goatee. No caption or script — devotional master-and-deity heads of the wai-khru repertoire, placed on the skin as protectors and lineage-sources of the sak-yant craft.

diagrams.title: Maha Yant Manual
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: ยันต์รูปกาย (figure/body-form yantra). A tall anthropomorphic yant built from paired, conjoined heart-lobes (ovals) stacked in three tiers — a broad base, a mid-body, and a small head — so the whole reads as a seated torso. Each lobe encloses a syllable in Khom (ขอม) sacred script, set in facing pairs. The figure is crowned by an unalom (อุณาโลม) spiral flame rising from a small finial with flanki

diagrams.title: Maha Yant Manual
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: ยันต์กรอบสี่เหลี่ยม (boxed / framed yantra). Two rectangular yant frames drawn with looped "knot" corners (each corner tied off in a small circle). The upper frame holds two words in Khom (ขอม) script, each finished with a wavy sara/tail stroke, with a Thai caption ("กันง"/kan-…) between the two frames. The lower, wider frame contains four identical repeated hook-strokes in a row — a reduplicated

diagrams.title: Maha Yant Manual
diagrams.genreLabel: Magic & Ritual · ไสยศาสตร์
diagrams.desc: ยันต์กลม / ยันต์วงกลม (circular wheel yantra). A round mandala-form yant: three concentric rings divided by radial spokes into cells, each cell holding one Khom (ขอม) syllable, so the syllable-string winds around the wheel. At the hub, four quadrants each carry a rosette of three grouped finger-strokes around a small central mark. The form is a yant klom of the kind used for all-round, encircling

diagrams.title: Ancient Traditional Medicine
diagrams.genreLabel: Medicine · ตำรายา
diagrams.desc: A printed section-divider (not an illustrated diagram): a near-blank leaf with a small header ภาควิชา (phaak wichaa, "the subject-division of") above a large three-line title เภสัชกรรม (phesatchakam, pharmacy / compounding of medicines). Faint show-through ghosts the margins. It marks where the traditional-medicine manual turns to its pharmacy/drug-compounding section.

diagrams.title: Ancient Thai Massage Manual
diagrams.genreLabel: Medicine · ตำรายา
diagrams.desc: A blank leaf — no figure, script or caption is present. Flagged as a diagram page in the scan but effectively empty (a page-break or verso in the massage manual); it carries no illustrated content to read.
